2. Verify 'No results' message displays
3. Verify 'Reset filters' button appears prominently
4. Click reset button
5. Verify product list restored
Expected Result:
- Reset button visible below no-results message
- Button prominently displayed
- Clicking restores full product list

Title: Reset Filters - Category Page Context Preserved
Steps:

2. Apply multiple filters
3. Click 'Reset filters' button
4. Verify still on same category page
5. Verify category products restored
Expected Result:
- Stays on same category page (NOT redirected to /catalog)

2. Apply filters on search results
3. Click 'Reset filters' button
4. Verify search term preserved
5. Verify filters cleared
Expected Result:
- Filters cleared
- Search term remains in search bar
- Search results restored (without filters)
- URL shows search term, no filter params

Title: Filter Chips - Individual Close vs Reset All
Steps:
1. Apply 3+ filters (mix facet + checkbox)
2. Close ONE chip via × button
3. Verify only that filter removed
4. Close another chip
5. Use Reset button for remaining filters
Expected Result:
- Individual close removes single filter only
- Other filters preserved after single close
- Reset button clears all remaining filters
- Product count updates after each action

Title: Filters - Browser Back/Forward Navigation
Steps:
1. Apply filter A
2. Apply filter B
3. Press browser Back button
4. Verify filter B removed
5. Press browser Forward button
6. Verify filter B restored
Expected Result:
- Browser history correctly tracks facet filter URL params
- Back button removes last filter
- Forward button restores filter
- Chips sync with browser navigation

Title: Filters - Page Refresh Preserves State
Steps:
1. Apply multiple filters
2. Refresh page (F5 or Ctrl+R)
3. Verify filters preserved from URL
4. Verify chips render correctly
5. Verify reset button works
Expected Result:
- Filter state persisted via URL params
- Chips render after refresh
- Checkboxes checked correctly
- Reset button functional after refresh

Title: Filters - Multi-select Within Facet Group
Steps:
1. Select 2+ values in same facet (e.g., Brand: HP + Epson)
2. Verify separate chips for each value
3. Verify facet header shows count
4. Close one chip via × button
5. Verify only that value removed
Expected Result:
- Individual chips per facet value
- Facet header shows selected count (e.g., Brand (2))
- Individual chip removal works
- Other values in same facet preserved
- Product count updates

Title: Facet Filter Badge - Size xs Renders Legibly
Preconditions: Category with faceted filters, products available
Steps:
1. Navigate to category page with facets
2. Apply a facet filter (e.g. Brand)
3. Locate the filter chip that appears
4. Observe the badge element showing the count or filter indicator
5. Verify the badge is readable and clearly visible
Expected Result:
- Badge renders at xs size (smaller than previous sm)
- Badge text is legible (not clipped or overflowing)
- Badge has sufficient visual contrast against background
- Badge shape and padding are correct

Title: Facet Filter Badge - Double-Digit Count Uses Correct Padding
Preconditions: Category with many filter values available
Steps:
1. Navigate to category with many filterable values
2. Apply 10 or more values from the same facet group
3. Observe the facet header badge showing the count
Expected Result:
- Badge shows double-digit count (e.g. '10', '12')
- Badge width expands to fit double-digit count (px-1 dynamic padding)
- Count not clipped or truncated
- Badge remains proportionally correct

Title: Category Selector Badge - Size xs Renders Correctly
Steps:
1. Navigate to a top-level category page that shows subcategory navigation
2. Locate the category selector component
3. Observe count badges on category items
Expected Result:
- Category item count badges render at xs size
- Badge count is readable
- No layout overflow from smaller badge
- Badge consistent in appearance across different category items

Title: Facet Filter Badge - WCAG Contrast at xs Size
Preconditions: Category with facets, browser contrast checker or DevTools
Steps:
1. Navigate to category page with facets
2. Apply a filter to show filter chip with badge
3. Use browser DevTools or accessibility checker to measure badge text contrast ratio
Expected Result:
- Badge text contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 against badge background (WCAG AA for normal text)
- Contrast maintained despite xs size reduction
- Badge meets WCAG 2.1 Level AA

Title: Compare Page Display
Preconditions: 2 or more products added to the compare bar
Steps:
1. Add 2 or more products to compare via category page
2. Click 'Compare' in the compare bar or navigate to /compare
3. Verify the compare page loads
4. Verify products are displayed side by side
5. Verify specs/attributes are shown in rows for comparison
Expected Result:
- Compare page loads at /compare or similar URL
- Products are displayed in a side-by-side column layout
- Product names, images, and prices are shown at the top of each column
- Specification attributes (e.g., size, weight, material, warranty) are listed in rows
- Differences between products are visually highlighted (if feature supported)
- Each product has an 'Add to Cart' button on the compare page

Title: Compare Limit
Preconditions: Category page with the compare feature active, currently comparing the maximum allowed products
Steps:
1. Add products to compare until the maximum is reached (check for a limit, typically 3 or 4)
2. Attempt to add one more product beyond the limit
3. Verify a limit message is displayed
4. Verify the extra product is not added
Expected Result:
- System enforces a maximum number of comparable products (e.g., 3 or 4)
- Attempting to add beyond the limit shows a message: 'You can compare up to X products' or similar
- The over-limit product is not added to the compare bar
- Compare bar still shows only the allowed maximum number of products

Title: Remove from Compare
Preconditions: 2 or more products added to compare, on /compare page
Steps:
1. Navigate to the compare page with 2+ products
2. Locate the remove button (X or Remove) on one product column
3. Click remove on one product
4. Verify the removed product's column disappears
5. Verify remaining products are still displayed
Expected Result:
- Remove/close button is available per product on the compare page
- Clicking remove eliminates that product's column from the comparison
- Remaining products are still shown correctly
- Compare bar count decrements to reflect the removal
- If only 1 product remains, the page may show a prompt to add more for comparison

Title: Compare Empty State
Preconditions: No products added to compare (fresh session or after clearing)
Steps:
1. Navigate to /compare with no products in the compare bar (clear compare if needed)
2. Observe the empty state on the compare page
Expected Result:
- Compare page loads without error even with no products
- Empty state UI is shown with a message: 'No products to compare' or similar
- A call-to-action is displayed: 'Browse catalog' or 'Add products to compare' link
- No broken layout or JavaScript errors
- User can easily navigate back to catalog to add products
